title: Outdoor Sofa with Armrests
summary: This DIY outdoor sofa is made out of pieces of 2x6 that are screwed together with pocket holes.
description: This DIY outdoor sofa is made out of pieces of 2x6 that are screwed together with pocket holes. The side panels double as armrests.

materials.name: Box of Kreg Blue-Kote screws (See tools list)
Wood putty or dowels, for filling pocket holes

steps.title: Cut the 2x6s
steps.body:
Cut 8 pieces of 2x6 at a length of 72".
Cut 8 pieces of 2x6 at a length of 30".

steps.title: Assemble the side panels
steps.body: Use pocket holes to connect the pieces. I placed the holes on the inside or under side of the pieces so that they would be less visible.

steps.title: Assemble the seat and back panels
steps.body: Use pocket holes to connect the pieces. I placed the holes on the inside or under side of the pieces so that they would be less visible. The seat panel has a middle piece that is perpendicular to provide strength. See image for details.

steps.title: Add the seat panel
steps.body: Placing the side panels upside down makes it easier to install the seat panel. I left 1-1/2" space between the front side of the side panels and the front edge of the seat panel.

steps.title: Add the back rest
steps.body: I left a 1/4" gap between the back rest and seat panel and set the angle to align with the corner of the side panels.

steps.title: Sand if desired, and seal
steps.body: Sand the sofa if desired, and then seal with an outdoor sealer. Enjoy!
